title: Multiplication & Division
objective: Students will explore multiplication & division concepts including Multiplication as repeated addition, Arrays for multiplication.

steps.content: Have you ever thought about Multiplication as repeated addition? Let's find out together!
Understand multiplication as repeated addition and grouping equal sets
Let's practice together! Explain that 3 groups of 2 is the same as 2 + 2 + 2
If you needs to count 4 bags with 3 apples in each, can they see that instead of counting all 12 one by one, they could add 3 + 3 + 3 + 3 — or say '4 groups of 3'?
Great job! Today we learned about Multiplication as repeated addition. Explain that 3 groups of 2 is the same as 2 + 2 + 2 Use objects to make equal groups and count the total
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Arrays for multiplication!
Use arrays to represent multiplication and division situations
Time to try this out: Build an array of 3 rows of 4 objects to show 3 × 4
If you sees eggs arranged in 3 rows of 4 in a box, can they use that grid layout to work out both '3 × 4 = 12' and '12 ÷ 3 = 4'?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Arrays for multiplication: Build an array of 3 rows of 4 objects to show 3 × 4 Read an array and state the total
